response.justification: Sarah brings 15+ years of progressive full-stack experience with strong alignment to the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, Docker, and Kubernetes are all present across recent roles. Her current position at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which is a rare and high-value match for the AI agent development requirement. A deep testing culture is evident through TDD advocacy, open-source testing libraries, and workshop facilitation. The main gaps are the absence of Nest.js and Vitest (core to the stated stack), no evident Kotlin experience, and limited explicit mention of authentication/authorization or PostgreSQL work.
strengths: Current hands-on AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Production TypeScript/Node.js/React across multiple roles, plus AWS CDK and Kubernetes, covers most of the required stack and cloud/containerization criteria
Deeply embedded testing culture: TDD practitioner, creator of open-source D-language testing libraries, and facilitator of multiple testing workshops
concerns: Nest.js is not mentioned anywhere in the resume despite being the primary backend framework in the target stack
Kotlin is absent from the skills list and recent experience; Java appears only in older embedded/robotic-arm contexts, not modern backend services
Vitest and Playwright are not referenced; testing framework expertise leans on custom tooling and older patterns rather than the specified modern JS testing suite

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience
